raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, my Citi card was charged {$240.00} by XXXX On XX/XX/year>, XXXX XXXX charged {$310.00} for a vehicle rental at XXXX XXXX. Within 24 hours, the vehicle developed severe XXXX XXXX  and was declared XXXX  and undrivable by the merchants own roadside technician. Despite repeated calls, XXXX XXXX was unreachable, forcing me to rent a replacement car and incur additional expenses. 
I previously filed a CFPB complaint that Citi denied, claiming the merchants refused credits. However, I have since reviewed Citis own Cardmember Agreement, which adopts the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ). It explicitly states that a cardholder may not have to pay for unsatisfactory goods or services when a good-faith effort to resolve with the merchant fails. Both conditions are clearly met : XXXX vehicle failed within 24 hours, and XXXX XXXX car was confirmed unsafe by their own technician. 
The agreement language is unambiguous : If you are dissatisfied with goods or services purchased with your card, and you have tried in good faith to correct the problem with the merchant, you may have the right not to pay the remaining amount due on them. 
Given I have been a Citi cardholder for nearly XXXX  years, averaging almost {$200000.00} in annual spend, it is petty and reprehensible for Citi to deny protection in a situation where a vehicle carrying my XXXX child was unsafe. 
I have attached Citis Cardmember Agreement ( personal AAdvantage version ) as evidence. I specifically requested Citi provide the Business AAdvantage World Elite Mastercard agreement that governs my account, but Citi has not done so. The personal agreement which Citi uses as a standard explicitly incorporates the Fair Credit Billing Act and confirms that cardholders may not have to pay for unsatisfactory goods or services after a good-faith effort with the merchant. Unless Citi can show that its business agreement affirmatively removes these protections ( which would be highly unusual and unfair ), these same obligations apply here. 
I have also re-attached all evidence from my prior CFPB complaint, now reorganized and labeled, so that both CFPB and Citi have a complete, clear record in one place.
